tools.name: shell_create
tools.description: Create a new PTY-backed shell session and return its id. Session starts detached; output is captured in a per-session ring buffer (default 64 KiB) until someone attaches and reads. Pass either argv (preferred — no shell-quoting hazard) or cmd (passed to /bin/sh -c).
tools.schema: {"type":"object","properties":{"argv":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"},"description":"argv vector. argv[0] is the executable."},"cmd":{"type":"string","description":"shell command line. Used only if argv is unset; runs under /bin/sh -c."},"env":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"},"description":"NAME=value pairs. Replaces, doesn't extend, the host environment."},"cwd":{"type":"string"},"cols":{"type":"integer","minimum":0,"maximum":65535},"rows":{"type":"integer","minimum":0,"maximum":65535},"buffer_bytes":{"type":"integer","description":"Per-session output ring capacity (4 KiB-4 MiB; default 64 KiB)."}}}

tools.name: shell_list
tools.description: List all live sessions in the runtime's PTY registry. Each entry has id, cmd, alive, attached, started_at, buffered (bytes ready to read), dropped (bytes lost to ring overflow), and exit_code if the session has ended.

tools.name: shell_attach
tools.description: Claim a session for read/write. Single-attach-at-a-time; fails with 'already attached' unless force=true. Force is the recovery path for 'previous attacher crashed without detaching'.

tools.name: shell_detach
tools.description: Release the attach lock. Session keeps running, output keeps buffering up to ring capacity. Re-attach later replays the buffered bytes.

tools.name: shell_write
tools.description: Write bytes to the session's stdin. Caller must be attached. Provide either data (UTF-8 string, newlines preserved) or data_b64 (base64 for raw bytes — Ctrl-C, escape sequences, binary).

tools.name: shell_read
tools.description: Read up to max_bytes from the session, blocking up to timeout_ms for new bytes. Returns the buffered ring contents first then live bytes. Returns eof:true when the session is dead and the ring is drained. data_b64 is always populated; data is populated when the bytes look like plain text.

tools.name: shell_signal
tools.description: Send a POSIX signal to the session's child process. Common values: 2=SIGINT (Ctrl-C), 9=SIGKILL, 15=SIGTERM (default). Doesn't require attach — Ctrl-C is out-of-band.

tools.name: shell_resize
tools.description: Set the PTY's window size. Needed for full-screen TUIs (vim, htop, less) to render correctly. Doesn't require attach.

tools.name: shell_destroy
tools.description: Terminate the session: SIGTERM, 2-second grace, then SIGKILL. Drops it from the registry. Idempotent.
